text: If today's north wind felt like a gift, that was the cold front clearing out the week's soup, and the weekend behind it is the good stuff. This afternoon is the only wrinkle: the official forecast has a few isolated showers after 4, and the European model is running much wetter than the American one, so keep half an eye on the sky if you're on the water late. Otherwise it's low 80s, dry, and the lake is sitting at 74 with waves a foot or less into Sunday, about as friendly as the broad lake gets. One caveat: Canadian wildfire smoke has air quality in the moderate range, so if you're sensitive, take the easy version of tonight's run. Sunset's 8:38 and worth a walk. Saturday and Sunday look like the two best lake days in a while; I'd get the swim in this weekend, because Tuesday turns hot with real storm potential behind it.
